visa_types.name: Corporate Visas
visa_types.documents_required:
General requirements
- Valid passport- valid for not less than 30 days
- Yellow fever certificate
- Flight bookings
- To attached copies of all S.A visas
- Payment of the prescribed fee
Note: Non- Ugandan Nationals- permits issued after July 2014 to be certified by internal affairs
- Submit duly completed application forms signed by the applicants
- Provide a corroborated statement demonstrating the need to employ foreign workers and the number of foreigners to be employed
- A certificate by the Department of Labor
- Proof of registration of the corporation with the:
- The South African Revenue Service 
- Unemployment Insurance Fund 
- Compensation Fund for Occupational Injuries 
- Companies and Intellectual Properties Commission, where legally required 
- The applicant for a corporate visa must provide proof that at least 60% of the total staff complement that are employed are citizens and permanent residents employed permanently in various positions.
- Provide a job description and remuneration for each foreign worker
- An undertaking to ensure that:
- the foreigner workers have valid passports
the foreign workers will be employed only in the specific positions for which the visa are issued
the foreign workers will leave South Africa on completion of their duties
- A cash deposit or guarantee for the foreign workers may be payable by the corporate entity or by the workers in the case of skilled workers
- Upon receipt of the approved number of workers, the DHA will issue the corporate visa and authorization certificates for each worker in terms of regulation 18(2) (b), then the corporate entity can start recruiting workers. Once a corporate visa has been issued to the applicant (entity), the applicant may then proceed to recruit workers, the workers will then apply for corporate worker certificates and these certificates are issued in line with the corporate visa for 3 years, but may not exceed the validity period of the corporate visa.
For more information  about the necessary documentation, click here  (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/HJ5dCodAKk9wFVMxfxfed/5918cb5358984154f95d5d4c77667476/SOUTH-AFRICA-LONG-TERM-VISA-APPLICATION-CHECKLIST.pdf)

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A corporate visa allows a corporate entity (e.g. a mine group, farmer, etc.) to employ a pre-determined number of skilled/semi-skilled/ unskilled workers. A corporate visa is issued for a period not exceeding three years.

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Corporate Visas]
The fee mentioned is per applicant
Please Note:
- The visa fee in USD is subject to change without notice.
- There is a service charge of USD 90 to be charged in UGX inclusive of VAT) levied per application over and above the visa fees.
- Visa fee (to be paid in cash – USD only) and service charge (to be paid in cash/ Mobile Money in UGX only) are to be paid at the bank implant located inside the Application Centre. Please carry a photocopy of the first page of your passport while paying the service fee and visa fee at the bank.
- There is no exemption for VFS Service charge.
- The above fees are non-refundable and non-transferable.

## Documents Required
General requirements
- Valid passport- valid for not less than 30 days
- Yellow fever certificate
- Flight bookings
- To attached copies of all S.A visas
- Payment of the prescribed fee
Note: Non- Ugandan Nationals- permits issued after July 2014 to be certified by internal affairs
- Submit duly completed application forms signed by the applicants
- Provide a corroborated statement demonstrating the need to employ foreign workers and the number of foreigners to be employed
- A certificate by the Department of Labor
- Proof of registration of the corporation with the:
- The South African Revenue Service 
- Unemployment Insurance Fund 
- Compensation Fund for Occupational Injuries 
- Companies and Intellectual Properties Commission, where legally required 
- The applicant for a corporate visa must provide proof that at least 60% of the total staff complement that are employed are citizens and permanent residents employed permanently in various positions.
- Provide a job description and remuneration for each foreign worker
- An undertaking to ensure that:
- the foreigner workers have valid passports
the foreign workers will be employed only in the specific positions for which the visa are issued
the foreign workers will leave South Africa on completion of their duties
- A cash deposit or guarantee for the foreign workers may be payable by the corporate entity or by the workers in the case of skilled workers
- Upon receipt of the approved number of workers, the DHA will issue the corporate visa and authorization certificates for each worker in terms of regulation 18(2) (b), then the corporate entity can start recruiting workers. Once a corporate visa has been issued to the applicant (entity), the applicant may then proceed to recruit workers, the workers will then apply for corporate worker certificates and these certificates are issued in line with the corporate visa for 3 years, but may not exceed the validity period of the corporate visa.
For more information  about the necessary documentation, click here  (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/HJ5dCodAKk9wFVMxfxfed/5918cb5358984154f95d5d4c77667476/SOUTH-AFRICA-LONG-TERM-VISA-APPLICATION-CHECKLIST.pdf)

## Photo Specifications
Sample Photograph: Below specs to be maintained at 45mm x 35mm

Specifications

The successful delivery of some of the department's services relies not only on our efforts. You have the responsibility of providing the Department with the correct documentation. Please find details below for all supporting documentation that the department requires:

Photographs

Photographs submitted for identity documents and passports must comply with the following specifications:
- Length - 45mm
- Width - 35mm
- The background of the photograph shall be plain and free of shadows and shall not have a white lining
- Only the head and shoulders of the person concerned shall be included in the photograph and the head from the chin to the top of the hair shall not be smaller 22mm and not larger than 25mm
Head Coverings:
- Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown.
- The person concerned shall be photographed without any head-gear or veil, and any other additions to the face which tend to alter the natural likeness shall be removed. (The Director-General may exempt any person or any category of persons from any of these requirements.)
Glasses and Head Covers

If you wear glasses:
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses.
- No dark tinted lenses or heavy frames
- Make sure that the frames do not cover any part of your eyes.
- Spectacles may be worn provided that the lenses do not make the eyes invisible. Spectacles with dark lenses may be worn if the eyes will appear distorted without them or if the person is blind
Expression:

The Photograph must:
- Show you looking at the Camera with a neutral expression and mouth closed
No obvious grinning, frowning or raised eyebrows.
- The face shall be a recognizable likeness of the person and the head, hair, eyebrows, eyes, nose and moustache or beard, if any, shall be sharply and clearly defined
- Neither the photograph nor the copies thereof may be defaced by holes, pencil or ink marks or in any other manner

## Processing Time
 The timelines given by South African High Commission in Uganda for the processing of each application is minimum 8 weeks from the day of submission at Visa Application Centers.
However visa applicants should bear in mind that each application is subject to an individual assessment and processing time may vary. High Commission Visa section may ask for additional documents which may require more additional working days for processing. 
Withdrawal of the Application.

																		   If the applicant wishes to withdraw his/her application after the submission of the application at VFS Centers in Uganda, below are the requirements.
- A withdrawal form is required       to be filled. This can be collected from the VFS Centre
- A covering letter mentioning       the reason for the withdrawal.
- A VFS ICR copy, a copy of       international passports bio –page

## Download Forms

Please fill in and complete visa application form with black ink pen
Authorisation Letter For Passport Collection
Bank Consent Letter

visa_types.name: Study Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
Two Coloured Recent (less than one month from the date of application) Photographs
Applicants should submit in person. (Children below the age of 18 are exempted)
Valid Passport with validity for no less than 30 days after the expiry of the intended visit
Valid Yellow Fever Vaccination Certificate
A complete and signed application form (BI-84 form) in black ink only.
Third Country (Destination) Visa
3 months local Bank Statement (Up to the Current date of submission) with bank seal and signed by bank authority
Bank Letter confirming Account Number, Account Name and Account Balance in figure and should indicate the bank email and contact number of concerned person in bank.
Foreign bank statement has to be stamped by the bank and certified by commissioner of oaths. (For that particular country)
Consent Letter  (Permitting the  South Africa High Commission to get information from the bank) 
Return Air Booking
Prescribed Visa and service Fee.
Minor Dependent Children accompanying applicant to submit:
- An Unbridged (Full) Birth Certificate
- In case of Unaccompanied Minor, consent from both the guardian with letters signed by both the parents.
- In the case of a minor, subject to guardianship or custody, proof of guardianship.
- In the case of an adopted minor proof of that adoption along with copies of parents/guardians identification.
- In case of one parental accompanying minor, written consent from the other parent.
- In case of minor a consent Affidavit form should be filled in and signed by both parents and stamped by the commissioner of oaths.
- All documents need to be presented at the center in original and a copy. Original will be seen and verified before returning back and copy will be stamped as seen by the center authority. Any document without original, the copy will be not stamped as “Original Seen” .
Kindly be informed that South Africa High Commission reserve the right to request for any additional documents from applicant.
For more information about the necessary documentation, click here

Overview
South African Transit visas are available “ONLY” to the following countries listed below: 
- BOTSWANA
- NAMIBIA
- LESOTHO
- MOZAMBIQUE
- SWAZILAND
- ZIMBABWE
Please Note: Transit Visa requirement must include the Visa of the neighboring  country where the applicant will be transiting to.

visa_types.name: Relatives Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
General requirements
- Valid passport- valid for not less than 30 days
- Yellow fever certificate
- Flight Reservations
- To attach copies of all S.A visas
- Payment of the prescribed fee
- Foreign nationals in Uganda must hold a long-term permit valid for at least one year, with a minimum of six months remaining before expiration. The permit must be endorsed in the passport and certified by internal affairs that it’s a true and genuine permit before submission.
- Applicants using company documents the following are required :Certified copies of certificate of registration, company form 7 or form 20, certified copy of certificate of incorporation and all ID copies of directors.
- The passport must be valid for 30 days after end of the intended visit
- A vaccination certificate, if required by the Act.
- Payment of the prescribed fee (not applicable for the spouse or dependent children of a South African citizen/permanent resident)
- A cash deposit equivalent value to a return or onward ticket (not required for the spouse or dependent children of South African citizen/permanent resident).
- A statement and/or documentation confirming the purpose and duration of your visit and proof that you are an immediate family member of a South African citizen/permanent resident (a full birth certificate for children, a marriage certificate in the case of a spouse and proof of kinship for others)
- A police certificate of good conduct from each country in which you resided for 12 months or longer since the age of 18 years
- Medical reports for yourself and all family members accompanying you
- Proof of South African citizenship or permanent residence of the relative in South Africa in the form of an identity document or passport, Municipal bills/body corporates all have to be certified by Police/Commissioner of South Africa.
- Proof availability of a minimum amount of R8500 per month per person for living expenses (this does not apply for a spouse or dependent minor child of a South African citizen/ permanent resident)
- The holder of a relative’s visa may not conduct work.
For more information  about the necessary documentation, click here  (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/HJ5dCodAKk9wFVMxfxfed/5918cb5358984154f95d5d4c77667476/SOUTH-AFRICA-LONG-TERM-VISA-APPLICATION-CHECKLIST.pdf)

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
As an immediate family member  of a South African citizen or permanent resident you may be issued a relative’s  visa once the prescribed financial assurance as contemplated in the Immigration  Act, 2002 (Act No. 13 of 2002) have been complied with. Relative’s visas are  issued for a maximum period of two years at a time. For a spouse and/or a  dependent child of a South African citizen or permanent resident the permit is  issued free of charge, whilst there is a fee for other relatives wishing to  join their family members in South Africa.

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Relatives Visa]
The fee mentioned is per applicant
Please Note:
- The visa fee in USD is subject to change without notice.
- There is a service charge of USD 90 to be charged in UGX inclusive of VAT levied per application over and above the visa fees.
- Visa fee (to be paid in cash – USD only) and service charge (to be paid in cash/ Mobile Money in UGX only) are to be paid at the bank implant located inside the Application Centre. Please carry a photocopy of the first page of your passport while paying the service fee and visa fee at the bank.
- There is no exemption for VFS Service charge.
- The above fees are non-refundable and non-transferable.

## Documents Required
General requirements
- Valid passport- valid for not less than 30 days
- Yellow fever certificate
- Flight Reservations
- To attach copies of all S.A visas
- Payment of the prescribed fee
- Foreign nationals in Uganda must hold a long-term permit valid for at least one year, with a minimum of six months remaining before expiration. The permit must be endorsed in the passport and certified by internal affairs that it’s a true and genuine permit before submission.
- Applicants using company documents the following are required :Certified copies of certificate of registration, company form 7 or form 20, certified copy of certificate of incorporation and all ID copies of directors.
- The passport must be valid for 30 days after end of the intended visit
- A vaccination certificate, if required by the Act.
- Payment of the prescribed fee (not applicable for the spouse or dependent children of a South African citizen/permanent resident)
- A cash deposit equivalent value to a return or onward ticket (not required for the spouse or dependent children of South African citizen/permanent resident).
- A statement and/or documentation confirming the purpose and duration of your visit and proof that you are an immediate family member of a South African citizen/permanent resident (a full birth certificate for children, a marriage certificate in the case of a spouse and proof of kinship for others)
- A police certificate of good conduct from each country in which you resided for 12 months or longer since the age of 18 years
- Medical reports for yourself and all family members accompanying you
- Proof of South African citizenship or permanent residence of the relative in South Africa in the form of an identity document or passport, Municipal bills/body corporates all have to be certified by Police/Commissioner of South Africa.
- Proof availability of a minimum amount of R8500 per month per person for living expenses (this does not apply for a spouse or dependent minor child of a South African citizen/ permanent resident)
- The holder of a relative’s visa may not conduct work.
For more information  about the necessary documentation, click here  (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/HJ5dCodAKk9wFVMxfxfed/5918cb5358984154f95d5d4c77667476/SOUTH-AFRICA-LONG-TERM-VISA-APPLICATION-CHECKLIST.pdf)

## Photo Specifications
Sample Photograph: Below specs to be maintained at 45mm x 35mm

Specifications

The successful delivery of some of the department's services relies not only on our efforts. You have the responsibility of providing the Department with the correct documentation. Please find details below for all supporting documentation that the department requires:

Photographs

Photographs submitted for identity documents and passports must comply with the following specifications:
- Length - 45mm
- Width - 35mm
- The background of the photograph shall be plain and free of shadows and shall not have a white lining
- Only the head and shoulders of the person concerned shall be included in the photograph and the head from the chin to the top of the hair shall not be smaller 22mm and not larger than 25mm
Head Coverings:
- Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown.
- The person concerned shall be photographed without any head-gear or veil, and any other additions to the face which tend to alter the natural likeness shall be removed. (The Director-General may exempt any person or any category of persons from any of these requirements.)
Glasses and Head Covers

If you wear glasses:
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses.
- No dark tinted lenses or heavy frames
- Make sure that the frames do not cover any part of your eyes.
- Spectacles may be worn provided that the lenses do not make the eyes invisible. Spectacles with dark lenses may be worn if the eyes will appear distorted without them or if the person is blind
Expression:

The Photograph must:
- Show you looking at the Camera with a neutral expression and mouth closed
No obvious grinning, frowning or raised eyebrows.
- The face shall be a recognizable likeness of the person and the head, hair, eyebrows, eyes, nose and moustache or beard, if any, shall be sharply and clearly defined
- Neither the photograph nor the copies thereof may be defaced by holes, pencil or ink marks or in any other manner

## Processing Time
 The timelines given by South African High Commission in Uganda for the processing of each application is minimum 8 weeks from the day of submission at Visa Application Centers.
However visa applicants should bear in mind that each application is subject to an individual assessment and processing time may vary. High Commission Visa section may ask for additional documents which may require more additional working days for processing. 
Withdrawal of the Application.

																		   If the applicant wishes to withdraw his/her application after the submission of the application at VFS Centers in Uganda, below are the requirements.
- A withdrawal form is required       to be filled. This can be collected from the VFS Centre
- A covering letter mentioning       the reason for the withdrawal.
- A VFS ICR copy, a copy of       international passports bio –page

## Download Forms

Please fill in and complete visa application form with black ink pen
Authorisation Letter For Passport Collection
Bank Consent Letter

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Exchange  Visas may be issued to foreigners who are not older than 25 years of age and  wish to participate in cultural, economic or social exchange programs  administered by an organ of the State or a public higher educational institution  in conjunction with an organ of a foreign state.
																  
																		  The  validity period of an exchange permit depends on the length of the program for  which it is issued and usually does not exceed 12 months. Exchange permits  cannot be renewed or extended. Should you  successfully apply for an exchange permit, you will not qualify for a temporary  residence permit until you have lived outside the Republic of South Africa for  at least 12 months.

- In case of one parental accompanying minor, written consent from the other parent.
- In case of minor a consent Affidavit form should be filled in and signed by both parents and stamped by the commissioner of oaths.
- All documents need to be presented at the center in original and a copy. Original will be seen and verified before returning back and copy will be stamped as seen by the center authority. Any document without original, the copy will be not stamped as “Original Seen” .

Sample Photograph: Below specs to be maintained at 45mm x 35mm

Specifications

The successful delivery of some of the department's services relies not only on our efforts. You have the responsibility of providing the Department with the correct documentation. Please find details below for all supporting documentation that the department requires:

Photographs

Photographs submitted for identity documents and passports must comply with the following specifications:
- Length - 45mm
- Width - 35mm
- The background of the photograph shall be plain and free of shadows and shall not have a white lining
- Only the head and shoulders of the person concerned shall be included in the photograph and the head from the chin to the top of the hair shall not be smaller 22mm and not larger than 25mm
Head Coverings:
- Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown.
- The person concerned shall be photographed without any head-gear or veil, and any other additions to the face which tend to alter the natural likeness shall be removed. (The Director-General may exempt any person or any category of persons from any of these requirements.)
Glasses and Head Covers

If you wear glasses:
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses.
- No dark tinted lenses or heavy frames
- Make sure that the frames do not cover any part of your eyes.
- Spectacles may be worn provided that the lenses do not make the eyes invisible. Spectacles with dark lenses may be worn if the eyes will appear distorted without them or if the person is blind
Expression:

The Photograph must:
- Show you looking at the Camera with a neutral expression and mouth closed
No obvious grinning, frowning or raised eyebrows.
- The face shall be a recognizable likeness of the person and the head, hair, eyebrows, eyes, nose and moustache or beard, if any, shall be sharply and clearly defined
- Neither the photograph nor the copies thereof may be defaced by holes, pencil or ink marks or in any other manner

General requirements
- Valid passport- valid for not less than 30 days

- To attach copies of all S.Avisas
- Payment of the prescribed fee

visa_types.name: Interviews
visa_types.documents_required:
Two Coloured Recent (less than one month from the date of application) Photographs
Applicants should submit in person.
Valid Passport with validity for no less than 30 days after the expiry of the intended visit
Valid Yellow Fever Vaccination Certificate
A complete and signed application form (DHA-84) in black ink only.
3 months local Bank Statement (Up to the Current date of Submission) with bank seal and signed by bank authority, if applicant is incurring expenses on his/her own
Bank Letter conforming Account Number, Account Name and Account Balance in figure and should indicate the bank email and contact number of concerned person in bank.
Applicants using company documents the following are required:
Certified copies of certificate of registration, company form 7 or form 20, certified copy of certificate of incorporation and all ID copies of directors.
Foreign nationals in Uganda must hold a long-term permit valid for at least one year, with minimum of six months remaining before expiration. The permit must be endorsed in passport and certified by internal affairs that it’s a true and genuine permit before submission.
Foreign bank statement has to be stamped by the bank and certified by commissioner of oaths. (For that particular country)
Consent Letter (Permitting the South Africa High Commission to get information from the bank)
Flight Reservations
Statement and /or documentation confirming the purpose and duration of visit.
Prescribed Visa and service fee.
Hotel booking copy in applicant’s name mentioning the duration of stay. (Direct Hotel Email Address)
If applicant staying at anybody’s house in South Africa, then recent three months Municipal bill/ body corporate supporting that house address is required.
Minor Dependent Children accompanying applicant to submit:
- An Unbridged (Full) Birth Certificate
- In case of Unaccompanied Minor, consent from both the guardian with letters signed by both the parents.
- In the case of a minor, subject to guardianship or custody, proof of guardianship.
- In the case of an adopted minor proof of that adoption along with copies of parents/guardian’s identification.
- In case of one parental accompanying minor, written consent from the other parent.
- In case of minor, a consent Affidavit form should be filled in and signed by both parents and stamped by the commissioner of oaths.
All documents need to be presented at the centre in original and a copy. Original will be seen and verified before returning back and copy will be stamped as seen by the centre authority. Any document without original, the copy will be not stamped as “Original Seen”.
Kindly be informed that South Africa High Commission reserves the right to request for any additional documents from applicant.
For more information about the  necessary documentation, click here  (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5Vu1RjPCOVmGmxLMJOQaI5/e8961df1c1fd20f165200c35b422d627/SOUTH-AFRICAN-SHORT-TERM-APPLICATION-CHECKLIST1.pdf)

Photographs

- The       background of the photograph shall be plain and free of shadows and shall       not have a white lining

Head Coverings:

visa_types.name: General Work Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
General requirements

- Valid passport- valid for not less than 30 days
- Yellow fever certificate
- Flight reservations
- To attach copies of all S.A visas
- Payment of the prescribed fee
- Foreign nationals in Uganda must hold a long-term permit valid for at least one year, with a minimum of six months remaining before expiration. The permit must be endorsed in the passport and certified by internal affairs that it’s a true and genuine permit before submission.
- Applicants using company documents the following are required :Certified copies of certificate of registration, company form 7 or form 20, certified copy of certificate of incorporation and all ID copies of directors.
- A duly completed (DHA-1738 Form 8) application form
- A passport valid for no less than 30 days after the expiry of intended visit
- Payment of the prescribed fee
- A vaccination certificate, if required by the Act
- Introduction letter
- Proof of financial means to cover envisaged living expenses in the Republic until the applicant receives a salary.
 
This should be in the form of:
 
Bank statements (Last three months Up to the Current date of the Submission)
- Medical report
- A police certificate of good conduct from each country in which you resided for 12 months or longer since the age of 18 years
- A written undertaking by the employer accepting responsibility for the costs related to the deportation of the applicant and his/ her dependent family members, should it become necessary.
- An application for a general work visa shall be accompanied by –
- A certificate from the Department of Labor
- Proof of qualifications evaluated by SAQA and translated by a sworn translator into one of the official languages of the Republic
- A contract of employment signed by both yourself and the employer.
- Full particulars of the employer including proof of registration with the Registrar of Companies
- An undertaking by the employer to inform the Director- General upon the employee no longer being in the employ of such employer or when employed in a different capacity or role.
- Proof of Accommodation
- All required documentation for a spouse and/or children if they intend to accompany you
For more information  about the necessary documentation, click here  (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/HJ5dCodAKk9wFVMxfxfed/5918cb5358984154f95d5d4c77667476/SOUTH-AFRICA-LONG-TERM-VISA-APPLICATION-CHECKLIST.pdf)

Specifications

The successful delivery of some of the department's services relies not only on our efforts. You have the responsibility of providing the Department with the correct documentation. Please find details below for all supporting documentation that the department requires:

Photographs

Photographs submitted for identity documents and passports must comply with the following specifications:
- Length - 45mm
- Width - 35mm
- The background of the photograph shall be plain and free of shadows and shall not have a white lining
- Only the head and shoulders of the person concerned shall be included in the photograph and the head from the chin to the top of the hair shall not be smaller 22mm and not larger than 25mm
Head Coverings:
- Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown.
- The person concerned shall be photographed without any head-gear or veil, and any other additions to the face which tend to alter the natural likeness shall be removed. (The Director-General may exempt any person or any category of persons from any of these requirements.)
Glasses and Head Covers

If you wear glasses:
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses.
- No dark tinted lenses or heavy frames
- Make sure that the frames do not cover any part of your eyes.
- Spectacles may be worn provided that the lenses do not make the eyes invisible. Spectacles with dark lenses may be worn if the eyes will appear distorted without them or if the person is blind
Expression:

The Photograph must:
- Show you looking at the Camera with a neutral expression and mouth closed
No obvious grinning, frowning or raised eyebrows.
- The face shall be a recognizable likeness of the person and the head, hair, eyebrows, eyes, nose and moustache or beard, if any, shall be sharply and clearly defined

General requirements

- Applicants using company documents the following are required :Certified copies of certificate of registration, company form 7 or form 20, certified copy of certificate of incorporation and all ID copies of directors.

- Introduction letter

- Proof of financial means to cover your day to day expenses as well as those of persons accompanying you (the medical permit holder), if any, in the form of: Bank statements (Last three months Up to the Current date of the Submission)

Overview
Medical  Treatment visas may be issued for a maximum period of six months at a time.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Medical  Treatment visas may be issued for a maximum period of six months at a time.

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Medical Treatment Visa]
The fee mentioned is per applicant
Please Note:
- The visa fee in USD is subject to change without notice.
- There is a service charge of USD 90 to be charged in UGX inclusive of VAT levied per application over and above the visa fees.
- Visa fee (to be paid in cash – USD only) and service charge (to be paid in cash/ Mobile Money in UGX only) are to be paid at the bank implant located inside the Application Centre. Please carry a photocopy of the first page of your passport while paying the service fee and visa fee at the bank.
- There is no exemption for VFS Service charge.
- The above fees are non-refundable and non-transferable.

## Documents Required
General requirements
- Valid passport- valid for not less than 30 days
- Yellow fever certificate
- Flight reservation
- To attached copies of all S.A visas
- Payment of the prescribed fee
- Foreign nationals in Uganda must hold a long-term permit valid for at least one year, with a minimum of six months remaining before expiration. The permit must be endorsed in the passport and certified by internal affairs that it’s a true and genuine permit before submission.
- Applicants using company documents the following are required :Certified copies of certificate of registration, company form 7 or form 20, certified copy of certificate of incorporation and all ID copies of directors.
- A duly completapplication form DHA-38
- The passport must be valid for 30 days after end of the intended visit
- Introduction letter
- Payment of the prescribed fee.
- Proof of financial means to cover your day to day expenses as well as those of persons accompanying you (the medical permit holder), if any, in the form of: Bank statements (Last three months Up to the Current date of the Submission)
- A letter from your medical practitioner or a medical institution, indicating the reasons/ necessity for treatment, the period of treatment and particulars of the treatment plans in the Republic of South Africa
- Details of the person or institution responsible for your medical expenses and hospital fees in form of a sponsorship letter, 3 months bank statement and a copy of their identifaction documents
- Proof of accomodation
- Should your medical scheme or employer not be liable for expenses incurred, proof of financial means or medical cover must be submitted
- Proof of sufficient financial means or provision for the costs indirectly related to the treatment.
- Particulars of persons accompanying you
- A copy of a return or onward air ticket.
- Certificate of good conduct for those 18 years and above and any other country one has lived or worked in for the period of one year and above when they obtained the age of 18 years
- A vaccination certificate, if required by law. 
Please note that a medical permit holder may not work or apply for work while in the country.
For more information  about the necessary documentation, click here  (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/HJ5dCodAKk9wFVMxfxfed/5918cb5358984154f95d5d4c77667476/SOUTH-AFRICA-LONG-TERM-VISA-APPLICATION-CHECKLIST.pdf)

## Photo Specifications

## 
Sample Photograph: Below specs to be maintained at 45mm x 35mm

Specifications

The successful delivery of some of the department's services relies not only on our efforts. You have the responsibility of providing the Department with the correct documentation. Please find details below for all supporting documentation that the department requires:

Photographs

Photographs submitted for identity documents and passports must comply with the following specifications:
- Length - 45mm
- Width - 35mm
- The background of the photograph shall be plain and free of shadows and shall not have a white lining
- Only the head and shoulders of the person concerned shall be included in the photograph and the head from the chin to the top of the hair shall not be smaller 22mm and not larger than 25mm
Head Coverings:
- Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown.
- The person concerned shall be photographed without any head-gear or veil, and any other additions to the face which tend to alter the natural likeness shall be removed. (The Director-General may exempt any person or any category of persons from any of these requirements.)
Glasses and Head Covers

If you wear glasses:
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses.
- No dark tinted lenses or heavy frames
- Make sure that the frames do not cover any part of your eyes.
- Spectacles may be worn provided that the lenses do not make the eyes invisible. Spectacles with dark lenses may be worn if the eyes will appear distorted without them or if the person is blind
Expression:

The Photograph must:
- Show you looking at the Camera with a neutral expression and mouth closed
No obvious grinning, frowning or raised eyebrows.
- The face shall be a recognizable likeness of the person and the head, hair, eyebrows, eyes, nose and moustache or beard, if any, shall be sharply and clearly defined
- Neither the photograph nor the copies thereof may be defaced by holes, pencil or ink marks or in any other manner

## Processing Time
The timelines given by South African High Commission in Uganda for the processing of each application is minimum 8 weeks from the day of submission at Visa Application Centers.
However, visa applicants should bear in mind that each application is subject to an individual assessment and processing time may vary. High Commission Visa section may ask for additional documents which may require more additional working days for processing.
Withdrawal of the Application.
If the applicant wishes to withdraw his/her application after the submission of the application at VFS Centers in Uganda, below are the requirements.
- A withdrawal form is required to be filled. This can be collected from the VFS Centre
- A covering letter mentioning the reason for the withdrawal.
- A VFS ICR copy, a copy of international passports bio –page

## Download Forms

Please fill in and complete visa application form with black ink pen
Authorisation Letter For Passport Collection
Bank Consent Letter

Return Air Booking
Prescribed Visa and service Fee.

- In the case of an adopted minor proof of that adoption along with copies of parents/guardians identification.
- In case of one parental accompanying minor, written consent from the other parent.
- In case of minor a consent Affidavit form should be filled in and signed by both parents and stamped by the commissioner of oaths.
- All documents need to be presented at the center in original and a copy. Original will be seen and verified before returning back and copy will be stamped as seen by the center authority. Any document without original, the copy will be not stamped as “Original Seen” .
